office_does: State representatives write and vote on state laws and budgets, oversee state government, and represent their legislative district.
race_blurb: Incumbent Shelley Kloba faces Bothell Deputy Mayor Jenne Alderks and first-time legislative candidates Jeff Lyon and Cliff Moon.

candidates.summary: Moon is a construction and small-business professional who emphasizes lower taxes, parental involvement, law enforcement, and legal immigration.
highlights: Emphasizes parental involvement in education and law enforcement [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly campaigns for lower taxes.

social.basis: His education platform emphasizes parental involvement and academic fundamentals.

experience.basis: He brings business and construction experience but no documented elected office.

candidates.summary: Lyon is a Navy veteran, former sheriff's deputy, and cybersecurity manager proposing broad tax relief, permanent school funding, and local housing control.
highlights: Proposes eliminating state taxes for families earning under $125,000 [S1]
Supports permanent school funding and restoring local control over housing rules [S1]

taxes.basis: He proposes eliminating state taxes for a large income group and requiring voter approval for an income tax.

local-control.basis: He explicitly proposes restoring local control over housing rules.

experience.basis: His military, law-enforcement, and technical record is outside elected office.

candidates.summary: Alderks is Bothell's deputy mayor and a former planning commissioner emphasizing transportation, housing, rights, parks, and local business.
highlights: Has served on the Bothell City Council since 2021 and is deputy mayor [S1]
Emphasizes housing, transportation, human rights, affordability, and local businesses [S1]

experience.basis: City-council, deputy-mayor, and planning-commission service provide a substantial local governing record.

candidates.summary: Kloba is a multi-term incumbent and former city councilmember focused on data privacy, AI safeguards, progressive taxation, green jobs, education, health, and civil rights.
highlights: Supports progressive taxation and education funding [S1][S2]
Supports data privacy, limits on data brokers, and reducing AI harms [S1][S2]
Offers state and municipal legislative experience [S1][S2]

taxes.basis: She explicitly supports progressive taxation to fund public priorities.

climate.basis: Green jobs provide a climate-action direction, though the dossier does not identify a detailed climate mechanism.

experience.basis: She has served in the Legislature since 2016 after municipal office.

tech.basis: Data-broker limits, privacy protections, and reducing AI harms directly favor stronger technology regulation.
